Build bridges to guide vehicles across gaps in this physics-based puzzle game. Construct bridges using different materials (road, wood, steel, springs) by placing segments between anchor points. Each material has different properties and costs. After building, test your bridge by starting the simulation. Complete the level when all vehicles safely cross without the bridge collapsing, staying within budget and piece limits. This test verifies that the player can place nodes, connect them with segments, and cycle through different materials. Place several nodes across the gap using Space, connecting them in a simple triangle pattern. Cycle through materials using A and D keys to test material switching. Build a basic stable bridge structure. Nodes should be placed at cursor positions, segments should connect sequential nodes, and material cycling should work. The bridge should be constructed without errors and be ready for simulation. Testing successful level completion scenario. This verifies that a well-designed bridge allows vehicles to cross safely and triggers the win condition. Build a strong, stable bridge using steel and wood supports in a triangular truss pattern. Connect both sides of the gap with a road segment on top. Start simulation and allow vehicles to cross completely. The bridge should remain stable under vehicle load. All vehicles should successfully cross to the destination. Game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state when all vehicles reach the goal. Testing bridge failure and game over condition. This verifies physics simulation detects structural failures and triggers appropriate game state. Build an intentionally weak bridge using only road segments with minimal support. Start simulation and observe as the bridge collapses under vehicle weight due to overstressed segments. Bridge segments should break when stress exceeds their capacity. The bridge should collapse realistically. Game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE when the bridge fails or vehicles fall. Testing budget constraint mechanics. This verifies that the cost system properly tracks spending and prevents exceeding budget limits. Attempt to place expensive steel segments repeatedly until approaching the budget limit. Try to exceed the budget by placing more segments than allowed by remaining funds. Each segment placement should deduct appropriate cost from remaining budget. The game should prevent placing segments when insufficient budget remains. Budget display should accurately reflect remaining funds. Testing spring material physics behavior. This verifies that spring segments behave differently from rigid materials with bouncy, elastic properties. Build a bridge section using spring material. Start simulation and observe how springs compress and expand under vehicle weight, providing bounce and flexibility unlike rigid materials. Spring segments should visibly compress when loaded and expand when unloaded. They should provide elastic support rather than rigid structure. Vehicles should bounce slightly when crossing spring sections. Testing pause functionality during simulation. This verifies game can be paused during active physics simulation and resume correctly. Poly Bridge

Build bridges to guide vehicles across gaps in this physics-based puzzle game. Construct bridges using different materials (road, wood, steel, springs) by placing segments between anchor points. Each material has different properties and costs. After building, test your bridge by starting the simulation. Complete the level when all vehicles safely cross without the bridge collapsing, staying within budget and piece limits.

Arrow Keys: Move cursor in build mode | Space: Place/Connect node in build mode | A/D: Cycle through materials (Road, Wood, Steel, Spring) | S: Start simulation/Test bridge | R: Restart level (from game over) | Enter: Start game (from start screen) | ESC: Pause/Unpause

test_3_ModeBtn.expected: Bridge segments should break when stress exceeds their capacity. The bridge should collapse realistically. Game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE when the bridge fails or vehicles fall.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing budget constraint mechanics. This verifies that the cost system properly tracks spending and prevents exceeding budget limits.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Attempt to place expensive steel segments repeatedly until approaching the budget limit. Try to exceed the budget by placing more segments than allowed by remaining funds.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Each segment placement should deduct appropriate cost from remaining budget. The game should prevent placing segments when insufficient budget remains. Budget display should accurately reflect remaining funds.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing spring material physics behavior. This verifies that spring segments behave differently from rigid materials with bouncy, elastic properties.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Build a bridge section using spring material. Start simulation and observe how springs compress and expand under vehicle weight, providing bounce and flexibility unlike rigid materials.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Spring segments should visibly compress when loaded and expand when unloaded. They should provide elastic support rather than rigid structure. Vehicles should bounce slightly when crossing spring sections.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing pause functionality during simulation. This verifies game can be paused during active physics simulation and resume correctly.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Build any functional bridge and start simulation. Press ESC to pause while vehicles are mid-crossing. Verify physics stops. Press ESC again to resume and verify simulation continues from paused state.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Pressing ESC during simulation should freeze all physics and vehicle movement. Paused overlay should appear. Pressing ESC again should resume exactly where left off with no physics glitches.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing multiple vehicle spawning and crossing sequence. This verifies that multiple vehicles can spawn and cross the bridge in sequence without interference.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Build a stable bridge that can support multiple vehicles. Start simulation and observe as vehicles spawn one after another and attempt to cross in sequence.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Vehicles should spawn at appropriate intervals. Multiple vehicles should be able to exist on the bridge simultaneously. Each vehicle should independently cross or fall based on bridge integrity.
